narrative.headline: 2022 Hyundai Elantra: seat belts top the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 93 complaints with NHTSA. Seat belts are the most reported area at 25%, followed by the electrical system (18%) and the forward collision avoidance system (16%). No deaths and no injuries are reported across any area. Only two complaints involve a serious event: one crash tied to the forward collision avoidance system and one fire tied to the engine.

issues.title: Seat Belts
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 23 reports (25%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Many reports tie back to a seat belt recall, so confirm any recall work has been completed.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 17 reports (18%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe multiple systems acting up at once, so check the dash gauges, camera, and power features.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 15 reports (16%), including 1 crash. No fires or injuries reported. Owners describe the automatic emergency braking activating unexpectedly.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 10 reports (11%), with 1 fire. No crashes or injuries reported. Watch for loss of acceleration and check engine warnings.

issues.title: Back Over Prevention
issues.blurb: The fewest reports here: 6 (6%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners mention the rear cross-traffic warning going inactive, so test it during a backup.
